meta.scope: Public internet sources worth considering for Role Foundry's software-engineering apprentice, with emphasis on frontend/product engineering, issue->patch work, code review, regression prevention, and honesty about evidence.
meta.honesty_note: This is a research inventory, not an approval to bulk-ingest everything listed. Manual-curation-only and not-suitable entries are included on purpose.

repo_context.role: software-engineering apprentice improving Role Foundry itself
current_gaps: accessibility/product-polish references grounded in public standards

sources.name: Playwright docs + examples
major_risks: Can drift into test-writing for its own sake instead of product work
Needs to stay tied to real Role Foundry UI flows

sources.name: Google Engineering Practices — code review guides
sources.licensing_access_status: Public. Repository license is CC BY 3.0.
major_risks: Should be converted into Role Foundry-local episodes and rubrics, not copied wholesale

sources.name: Alpine.js repo + docs
sources.licensing_access_status: Public MIT repo for code/docs, but GitHub issue/PR/review text should not be assumed to inherit that license for downstream training.
major_risks: Can drift into framework-internal work instead of Role Foundry product tasks

sources.name: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
sources.licensing_access_status: Public. Documents are licensed under the W3C Software and Document License.
major_risks: Can become generic accessibility trivia unless tied to specific Role Foundry screens

sources.name: MDN Web Docs
sources.licensing_access_status: Public. MDN prose is CC BY-SA; newer code samples are CC0 and older samples are mixed/historical.
major_risks: Reference prose is weaker than task-shaped examples for this role

sources.name: web-platform-tests (WPT)
major_risks: Can drag the curriculum toward browser-spec minutiae
Best added after a stable Role Foundry browser-test lane exists

sources.name: SWE-bench / SWE-bench Verified
sources.licensing_access_status: Public MIT harness/dataset packaging, but underlying task instances inherit mixed upstream repo realities and heavy infra assumptions.
major_risks: Broad and backend-heavy relative to current Role Foundry work

sources.name: Raw GitHub issue / PR / review-comment corpora
sources.licensing_access_status: Public access does not equal clean downstream training rights. GitHub ToS says users own the content they post; API use is separately governed.
major_risks: bad incentive to train on arguments instead of fixes

top_3_plan.first_step: Author 4-6 episodes tied to existing Role Foundry screens and require failing check -> fix -> passing rerun receipts.
Author 6-8 tiny review-only episodes from Role Foundry-local diffs scored for user risk detection, test/doc asks, scope control, and uncertainty honesty.
Pick at most 10 merged Alpine.js issues/PRs, keep only clearly licensed code/diff context, rewrite the first 3 into original Role Foundry episodes.

best_immediate_next_move: Add the Playwright regression mini-pack first.
